behavior_summary: This script implements a guard NPC that can be examined, interacted with (talked to), revived/respawned by area entry and scheduled logic, and runs small asynchronous behaviors.
GUARD5::look reports a short descriptor: if the NPC is dead it barks 'dead guardsman', otherwise 'guardsman'.

U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea acts as an area-entry trigger: if the guard is dead it clears the dead/combat state and teleports the guard to fixed coordinates; if alive it launches asynchronous behavior spawns so the guard can resume active behaviors.
U8P_GUARD5::schedule is a periodic/scheduling handler that may spawn a recovery/respawn behavior and clear the dead flag when schedule conditions are met.

U8P_GUARD5::use implements a multi-branch dialog/interact routine: it plays an initial bark, inspects several global flags to form an interaction counter, branches into panic/doomsday vs. normal dialog, updates persistent guard flags (guard5Met, guard5Pissed, guard5Thanked), presents a question menu (Item::ask) and responds to player choices, and spawns follow-up asynchronous behaviors.
notable_control_flow: GUARD5::look: single conditional branch on Npc::isDead -> two bark outcomes then suspend/return.
U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea: conditional on Npc::isDead. If dead -> Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat, Npc::teleport to fixed coords. Else -> spawn two behavior methods asynchronously.

U8P_GUARD5::schedule: prepares spawn parameters for a scheduled behavior (METHOD::143A), inspects the spawn result, and if a particular result is observed clears dead state and spawns a recovery handler (METHOD::133F).

U8P_GUARD5::use: complex, multi-stage branching. It builds an interaction counter from global flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) — if counter>1 or missing hasHeart triggers panic/doom branches that bark alarming lines and spawn METHOD::1090. Otherwise performs friendlier dialog, uses randomness for variation, conditionally sets/clears guard5Pissed and guard5Met, offers a question menu via Item::ask, and sets guard5Thanked in the thank-you branch.
open_questions: The precise behavior/purpose of spawn targets (METHOD::091C, ::15E7, ::143A, ::133F, ::0BCF, ::1090) is inferred (animations/AI/respawn) but not explicitly known.
FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 are invoked during GUARD5::use; their exact side-effects or data they manipulate are unclear (they appear to influence the local interaction counter or global state).
Exact timing/coordinate semantics and parameter meanings for Npc::teleport parameters (param1, param2) and schedule spawn parameter layout are not fully documented.
The full set of dialog text strings (bark content beyond summarized tokens) and language/localization boundaries are not exhaustively enumerated from the disassembly.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD5::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player 'U8P_GUARD5::look' action by announcing a short descriptor for the guard: 'dead guardsman' if the NPC is flagged dead, otherwise 'guardsman'.
entry_conditions: Event 0: triggered when a player/actor looks at or inspects this guard NPC (area-of-interest/U8P_GUARD5::look interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_GUARD5::look handler that reports a brief descriptor depending on NPC dead state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry fast trigger: revives/teleports a dead guard or spawns alive-behavior routines when an actor enters the trigger area.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler: manages timed respawn/recovery behaviors and clears dead state when schedule conditions are met.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::use
function_map.role: Player interaction/talk handler: complex dialog tree, state flag updates, question menu, and spawn follow-ups.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: query whether a given NPC is flagged as dead.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: make an NPC/item emit a short text line to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: clear the dead flag on an NPC (revive).

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: clear an NPC's in-combat flag/state.

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: teleport the NPC to given coordinates/orientation with extra params.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: asynchronously start another method/process (used to run AI/animation/respawn behaviors).

function_map.name: Item::ask
function_map.role: Engine/GM API: present a choice list to the player and return the selected string.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Utility: provide random numbers for dialog variation.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Project-specific external routine invoked during interaction; purpose inferred to affect interaction counter or inventory/global manager.

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Project-specific external routine invoked during interaction; purpose inferred to affect interaction counter or inventory/global manager.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the guard is dead to select the appropriate bark string.
Emit the short description string to the player ('dead guardsman' or 'guardsman').
Decide whether to revive/teleport the guard or to spawn alive behaviors when an actor enters the area.
Clear the NPC dead flag (revive) on area entry if dead.
Clear in-combat status when reviving the guard on area entry.
Teleport the revived guard to a fixed location/orientation after reviving.
Launch asynchronous behavior methods (e.g., METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7) to resume NPC activity when alive.
Schedule or spawn a periodic behavior (METHOD::143A) and later spawn a recovery handler (METHOD::133F) when conditions are met.
Clear dead state as part of scheduled recovery/respawn workflow if spawn result indicates recovery should occur.
Play initial and branch-specific dialog lines during player interaction.
Start follow-up asynchronous behaviors (METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090 and other METHOD::XXXX) as a result of dialog branches and interaction.
Project-specific call used during interaction; likely influences the interaction counter or checks/consumes a free-state resource.
Project-specific call used during interaction; likely influences the interaction counter or checks/consumes a free-state resource.
Present a choice menu to the player and obtain the selected question string for dialog branching.
Introduce small random variation in greeting/dialog selection.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used to increment the local interaction counter in GUARD5::use; indicates an external/freeable status affecting dialog tone.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Checked in GUARD5::use to influence panic/doomsday branches and to increment the interaction counter.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used to increment the interaction counter in GUARD5::use; signals another free-related condition affecting dialog.

reads.name: guard5Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether to use first-meet dialog; persists whether the player has previously met this guard.

reads.name: guard5Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose an angry/pissed dialog branch; influences subsequent dialog states.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used in the 'Where am I?' response to determine whether to mention the palace/Devon-specific location.

reads.name: guard5Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read to avoid re-running a thank-you dialog sequence when the player has already triggered it.

writes.name: guard5Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set or cleared by dialog branches in GUARD5::use to remember whether the guard is angry at the player for future interactions.

writes.name: guard5Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player triggers first-meet dialog branches; marks that the guard has been met before and changes future greetings.

writes.name: guard5Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard thanks the player for a deed (inferred: removal of Mordea); prevents repeating the thank-you sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a guard NPC that can be examined, interacted with (talked to), revived/respawned by area entry and scheduled logic, and runs small asynchronous behaviors.
